title: Account-specific API version divergence (new response fields, strict-deserialization risk)
description: Two new fields, account_tier and tier_features, are added to the GET /members/{id}/benefits response. From a pure OpenAPI contract perspective, adding fields to a response is backward-compatible. However, services that consume this endpoint using sealed Kotlin data classes or Jackson with DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=true will throw a deserialization exception when they encounter the new fields. This is a practical breaking change for strict consumers even though the OpenAPI spec change is additive. The risk is particularly relevant for account-type-specific services that may have tightly-bound deserialization models.

api_changes.reason: Adding a new field to a response is backward-compatible per OpenAPI spec. Lenient JSON deserializers ignore unknown fields by default.
api_changes.caveat: Consumers using sealed Kotlin data classes or Jackson with FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=true will throw UnrecognizedPropertyException at runtime.

api_changes.reason: Adding a new array field to a response is backward-compatible per OpenAPI spec. Lenient deserializers will ignore it.
api_changes.caveat: Strict-deserialization consumers (sealed data classes, FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES) will break when this field appears in the response payload.

expected.notes: Consumers should audit their JSON deserialization configuration. Services using @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true) or equivalent are safe. Services without it may fail silently or throw exceptions depending on framework defaults.
